approach: Systematic TPflash benchmark using NeqSim with SRK EOS across 6 natural gas families (lean gas, rich gas, gas condensate, CO2-rich, wide-boiling, sour gas). 1664 total cases with Dirichlet-perturbed compositions and log-spaced PT grids. Timing from 3 repeated trials per case.
conclusions: NeqSim's TPflash achieves 100.0% overall convergence across 1664 cases spanning 6 natural gas families. Best performance: co2 rich (100.0%). Most challenging: co2 rich (100.0%). Median computation time: 0.09 ms. Two-phase conditions comprise 22.4% of the test matrix.

figure_captions.fig1_convergence_by_family.png: TPflash convergence rate and two-phase case distribution across 6 fluid families (SRK EOS, 1664 total cases).
figure_captions.fig2_convergence_maps.png: Convergence success/failure mapped in temperature-pressure space. Green: single-phase converged; blue: two-phase converged; red cross: failed to converge.
figure_captions.fig3_timing_distribution.png: CPU timing distribution: (left) box plots by fluid family; (right) overall histogram with median and 95th percentile markers.
figure_captions.fig4_beta_distribution.png: Distribution of vapor fraction (beta) in two-phase cases by family.
figure_captions.fig5_time_vs_components.png: Median flash computation time vs number of components, showing expected scaling with system size.
figure_captions.fig6_near_critical_analysis.png: Near-critical analysis: (left) timing by phase fraction region; (right) timing vs pressure colored by number of phases.
